core_principles: Fusion over Imitation: Slack structure x VS Code utility x Cyberpunk aesthetics
Emotion First: The user feels like a 'Commander' in a high-tech control room
Content is King: Code and conversation are the heroes; UI recedes into the void
Negative Space: Dense data needs breathing room; use padding to separate logic flows
hall_of_shame: Center-aligned everything (we use asymmetric, functional layouts)

layout_structure.spacing_system: Comfortable density. Use p-4 for tight UI, p-8/p-12 for landing sections.

buttons.primary: bg-zinc-100 text-zinc-900 hover:bg-zinc-200 shadow-[0_0_15px_rgba(255,255,255,0.1)] transition-all font-medium

cards.style: bg-zinc-900/40 backdrop-blur-md border border-zinc-800/60 hover:border-zinc-700 transition-colors

inputs.style: bg-zinc-950 border-zinc-800 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-zinc-700 focus:border-zinc-700 placeholder:text-zinc-600 font-mono text-sm

chat_bubbles.ai: bg-zinc-900 border border-zinc-800 text-zinc-100 mr-auto rounded-2xl rounded-tl-sm
chat_bubbles.system: bg-transparent text-zinc-500 text-xs text-center w-full py-2 italic

sidebar.style: border-r border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-900/95 backdrop-blur flex flex-col h-screen

rules: Ensure text-to-background contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1
Use explicit focus states (ring-2 ring-blue-500/50) for keyboard navigation
Interactive elements must be at least 44x44px target size

instructions_to_main_agent: DO NOT use gradients for text.
DO NOT use pure black backgrounds; use Zinc-950 for depth.
Implement a 'Terminal' aesthetic for the code observation panel (monospaced, dark, simple).
Use distinct border colors or tiny avatar indicators to differentiate the 4 AI models.
The 'Connect' button for Google OAuth should be prominent but adhere to the dark theme (e.g., White button with Google 'G' logo).
Ensure the Chat UI feels 'real-time' - stick the scroll to bottom when new messages arrive.
universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
